product_identity.name: Crewly
product_identity.type: social trip coordination and pooled funding platform
product_identity.category: travel collaboration, group finance, and social coordination
product_identity.core_problem: Group trips are difficult to coordinate because planning, pooled funds, receipts, private side expenses, and updates are scattered across disconnected tools.
product_identity.core_promise: Crewly gives a trip organizer one place to coordinate the trip, collect support, track spending transparently, and manage private subgroup activity without exposing sensitive details publicly.
product_identity.primary_user_outcome: A trip organizer successfully launches a trip, gets the right people into the group, collects funds, tracks shared and subgroup expenses, and keeps members and supporters informed at the right privacy level.
secondary_outcomes: Trip members can actively contribute receipts, expenses, event updates, and photos.
Public supporters can contribute to selected trips without accessing private group details.
Private subgroup participants can track side-event expenses and payment status without cluttering the main trip ledger.
International travelers can understand receipts via translation and display-only currency conversion.

strategic_intent.why_now: Trip coordination increasingly spans public support, private planning, receipts, shared memories, and side-event spending, but mainstream tools split those needs across separate apps.
what_must_be_true: Organizers trust Crewly to centralize trip setup and financial visibility.
Members are willing to participate in shared financial logging and content contribution.
Supporters are comfortable contributing without seeing private details.
Users understand the distinction between trip-wide funds and private event balances.
required_user_behaviors: Organizers create and maintain the trip hub.
Members upload receipts, expenses, photos, and event activity.
Supporters use the public trip surface to contribute.
Event creators keep side-event tracking scoped to the right participants.
initial_exclusions: In-app peer-to-peer settlement for private events
Automatic rollup of private event balances into the trip ledger
Advanced fraud, dispute, and reimbursement tooling

assumptions.statement: Trip contributions are support toward the trip rather than lending or equity.
assumptions.impact: Defines the MVP payment posture and contributor expectations.

assumptions.statement: Organizer-controlled payout is acceptable for MVP if ledger transparency is clear.
assumptions.impact: Enables a simpler first payment model.

assumptions.statement: Display-only currency conversion is sufficient for shared trip comprehension.
Approved members can freely create private events without causing confusing or abusive subgroup sprawl.
assumptions.impact: Shapes event permissions and moderation scope.
